plan_metadata.name: Feature DA No Action Add OAuth Auth Configurations

steps.description: Type the URL `'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neil-yechenwei/uitest/refs/heads/main/Spec_add_auth_oauth_github.yaml'` into the file path input field at the top of the "OpenAPI Document" window in Visual Studio Code, and confirm the entry by pressing 'Enter'.
Press Enter to confirm the URL input in the "OpenAPI Description Document" address bar in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the blue "Clear All" button in the dropdown for operation selection within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension in Visual Studio Code, to deselect all chosen APIs.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects' as the project location in Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key in the Visual Studio Code application to open the Command Palette from the main Explorer view.
Type 'Add configurations to Support' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top of the interface.
Press Enter to select "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Configurations to Support Actions with Authentication" from the VS Code command palette.
Press Enter to confirm the selection of "ai-plugin.json" in the "Import Manifest File" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Type "oauth2" into the input field of the "Enter the Name of Auth Configuration" dialog box in Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter to confirm the input "oauth2" in the "Enter the Name of Auth Configuration" prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "OAuth" authentication option in the "Authentication Type" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type 'https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize' into the "Enter the OAuth Authorization URL" input box at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm the OAuth authorization URL input ("https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize") in the VS Code command palette prompt.
Type 'https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token' into the input field of the "Enter the OAuth Token URL" prompt at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the Enter key to submit the OAuth token URL in the "Enter the OAuth Token URL" input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press 'Enter' to confirm input in the "Enter the OAuth Refresh URL" prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Type text 'repo: Read repos' into the "Enter the OAuth Scope" input field in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Press the Enter key to confirm the "repo: Read repos" input in the OAuth Scope prompt within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "No" option in the "Enable PKCE for OAuth?" dropdown prompt within the Visual Studio Code interface.

Type text 'fakefakefakefakeid1' into the "Input client ID for OAuth registration in OpenAPI Description Document" prompt within Visual Studio Code, during the Provision workflow for a Microsoft 365 Agent.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the input value "fakefakefakefakeid1" in the "Input client ID for OAuth registration in OpenAPI Description Document" prompt within Visual Studio Code.

Type text 'fakefakefakefakefakefakefakefakefakefake' into the "Input client secret for OAuth registration in OpenAPI Description Document" password field prompt in Visual Studio Code during the Microsoft 365 Agents provisioning workflow.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input of the client secret in the "Input client secret for OAuth registration in OpenAPI Description Document" prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Confirm" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit OAuth registration dialog within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair at the top of the interface.
Click the "4 New Notifications" icon in the bottom-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to open recent alerts.
Click inside the "Message Copilot" input box within the vscuse_app_EQyVk chat window in the M365 Copilot web interface to focus the text field for entering a new message.

parameters.text: List repositories for the authenticated user

steps.description: Type the text "List repositories for the authenticated user" into the main message input box at the bottom center of the "M365 Copilot" web chat interface while the "vscuse_app_pcbqj" agent is selected.

Press the Enter key in the M365 Copilot chat interface to submit the query "List repositories for the authenticated user" within the agent, activating the send button at the lower right of the input box.
@assertion there is a button containing "Sign in to GitHub v3 REST API"
